data.title: Coordinate human walkthrough by a reviewer not involved in authoring; confirm path README -> /dso:onboarding is followable
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Purpose
Obtain a walkthrough confirmation from a team member not involved in authoring the docs. This is a manual step — the sub-agent coordinates the request and records the outcome.

## File Impact
- No file changes in normal flow; only edits if the walkthrough surfaces concrete blockers (then edit README.md / INSTALL.md accordingly and re-request confirmation).

## Implementation Steps
1. Wait for all authoring stories (3028-d955, 6845-09e4, 9eeb-8802, 4cb3-5553) to close.
2. Prepare a short walkthrough request describing what the reviewer should do:
   - Start at repo root README.md
   - Follow any links or next steps
   - Reach the /dso:onboarding entry point
   - Note any step that requires prior context not present in the docs
3. Post the walkthrough request as a comment on the ticket for a team member not involved in authoring. Because this requires a human reviewer who did NOT author the docs, the sub-agent cannot self-approve — it must pause and surface this dependency to the orchestrator.
4. When the reviewer responds:
   - If they confirm the path is followable without additional context — add their confirmation as a comment and mark the task complete.
   - If they report a specific blocker — create a follow-up bug ticket for the issue; once fixed, re-request walkthrough.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci
- [ ] Walkthrough confirmation comment exists on this ticket from a reviewer not listed as task author
  Verify: .claude/scripts/dso ticket show $(cat /tmp/self-ticket-id 2>/dev/null || echo '160d-795f') | grep -qi 'walkthrough.*confirm\|confirmed.*path\|followable'
